steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: "Only recently" signals a new discovery. Genetic research did something to the ancestor of summer squash — it made that ancestor known. The blank must mean discovered or made known.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
A. Revealed means made previously unknown information known. Substituting: "genetic research only recently revealed its ancestor to be the wild johnny gourd" — the research uncovered and disclosed an identity that was not known before. A is correct.

B. Expanded means made larger or broader. Research cannot expand an ancestor — and the passage is about identifying, not enlarging, a relationship. Eliminated.

C. Encouraged means promoted or gave support to something. Research cannot encourage an ancestor to exist. This doesn't fit the context of a discovery. Eliminated.

D. Petitioned means formally requested something from an authority. Genetic research makes discoveries; it does not file requests. Eliminated.
